title: Yemeni children at the heart of Houthi war machine
subhead: Since the coup at the end of 2014, Yemeni rebels have forcibly recruited thousands of minors each year, sending them to the front lines to fight against the government and Saudi Arabia.

title: After two diplomats were violently detained in Tehran, France expresses concern for its nationals in Iran

Bruno Cotte, magistrate: 'Contrary to what Marco Rubio claims, the International Criminal Court is neither right-wing nor left-wing'
subhead: The US Secretary of State's campaign to dismantle the ICC marks an assault on international law that should prompt much stronger reactions from its founders, especially the Europeans, argues the former ICC chamber president for Le Monde.

title: Bernard Arnault, generous patron of the arts and lover of tax breaks
subhead: 'Bernard Arnault's Empire' (5/6). France's richest man is also one of Europe's leading art collectors. Through the Fondation Louis Vuitton, he combines his passion for the arts with highly lucrative tax arrangements.

title: Chanel's XXL cruise ship in May 2018
subhead: 'Behind the scene' (2/6). Fashion houses often use runway shows as opportunities for particularly spectacular set designs. In May 2018, Karl Lagerfeld docked a life-size cruise ship under the glass dome of the Grand Palais in Paris for Chanel's cruise collection show.

title: My life as a Russian exile in Paris: 'France is an improved version of the USSR'
subhead: 'A Russian in Paris' (2/6). After arriving in France in 2022, journalist Elizaveta Osetinskaya settled in Paris' 16th arrondissement. She especially praises the French school system, which her daughter Bella now benefits from.

How Macron narrowly averted his environment minister's resignation
subhead: Monique Barbut, who said she intended to quit the government on Tuesday, July 21, after an agricultural law she contested was adopted, ended up staying on in her role on Wednesday. The twist added a colorful note to the political crisis.

title: Despite living 'like a walking dead man,' South Africa's Thabiso Zulu continues crusade against ANC corruption
subhead: For nearly a decade, the 44-year-old man has lived like a hunted animal. He sleeps in a different place almost every night and only shares meeting locations at the last moment.

title: My life as a Russian exile in Paris: 'You can't fight French bureaucracy'
subhead: 'A Russian in Paris' (1/6). Journalist Elizaveta Osetinskaya, who came to France in 2022 because of her opposition to the war in Ukraine, describes the difficulties she has faced with the prefecture while barely speaking the language.
